title: Anthropic frontier model provider
description: ModelProvider implementation for Claude 4.6+ class via Anthropic's HTTP API. Hosts shared frontier-tier helpers (FrontierDefaults, VisionImage, validateToolArgs, lookupTool) that the OpenAI provider also consumes. Vision support per model capability metadata. Frontier tier defaults: lower temperature, smaller observation budgets, tighter retry budgets vs the Qwen3.6 path. Web-compatible (HTTP only, no dart:io).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `AnthropicModelProvider` class is defined in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/anthropic/anthropic_provider.dart` and implements `ModelProvider` from .14; `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` passes
- [ ] `AnthropicModelProvider.decide(PromptPayload, ActionSchema)` issues a POST to `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` (or override URL) with `tools: [...]` derived from `PromptPayload.tools`, and parses the first `tool_use` content block into a `ModelDecision` whose `action.tool` matches the tool's dotted name and whose `action.args` is the block's `input` map
- [ ] When the response contains zero `tool_use` blocks, `decide()` throws `SchemaRejection` whose `validationError` reads `no tool_use block in response` and `rawOutput` contains the raw response body
- [ ] When the parsed `tool_use` block produces a JSON envelope that fails `ActionSchema.validate()`, `decide()` re-throws the `SchemaRejection` raised by `ActionSchema.validate()` unchanged (no swallow, no retry inside the provider)
- [ ] When `PromptPayload.userMessages` includes an entry whose `type == 'image'` with `source.data` (base64 PNG), the request body's `messages[].content` for that turn includes an Anthropic `{type: 'image', source: {type: 'base64', media_type: 'image/png', data: ...}}` block built by the shared `VisionImage` helper
- [ ] `FrontierDefaults` constant exposes `temperature: 0.2`, `maxTokens: 4096`, `maxObservationBytes: 6144`, `maxRetries: 1`, and is consumed by `AnthropicModelProvider` for request defaults
- [ ] `validateToolArgs(ToolDescriptor, Map<String, dynamic>)` validates a tool's args against its `inputSchema` using the same `package:json_schema` draft-07 path as `ActionSchema`, throwing `SchemaRejection` on mismatch
- [ ] `lookupTool(List<ToolDescriptor>, String wireName)` returns the matching `ToolDescriptor` for an Anthropic-encoded wire name (dotted name with `.` replaced by `_`); returns `null` when no match exists
- [ ] `AnthropicModelProvider.capabilities` reports `vision: true` when constructed with a model in `{'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'claude-opus-4-6'}` and `vision: false` for explicitly text-only model ids; `preserveThinking: false`; `supportsToolUse: true`; `maxContext: 200000`
- [ ] `FrontierDefaults`, `VisionImage`, `validateToolArgs`, and `lookupTool` are exported from `package:exploration_agent` (via the provider barrel) so `.37` can import them without reaching into `src/`
- [ ] Mock-client unit tests cover: happy path (single tool_use block parsed into `ModelDecision`), missing-tool_use-block path (throws SchemaRejection), screenshot turn (request body contains image block with base64 payload), and ActionSchema-rejection path (validator's exception escapes unchanged)
- [ ] Package `exploration_agent` remains web-compatible: a test under `test/provider/anthropic/` walks the new sources and asserts none import `dart:io`

title: Perception-action loop driver with budgets and failure handling
description: The orchestrator. Per turn: stabilize ‚Üí capture core ‚Üí capture plugin fragments ‚Üí diff ‚Üí build prompt ‚Üí decide ‚Üí validate (‚â§3 retries) ‚Üí act (host or plugin extension) ‚Üí notify plugins via onActionExecuted ‚Üí persist. Enforces per-turn (30s) and per-session (50 turns / 15min) budgets. Implements all PRD ¬ß17 failure modes: stability budget expired, invalid action retries, malformed model retries, three-failed-turns ‚Üí harness_error: agent_stuck, plugin exception isolation + auto-disable after 3 failures, runtime app errors don't end session, VM connection lost preserves trajectory, binding-not-initialized exit, budget exhaustion clean end, voluntary done(reason). PRD ¬ß10, ¬ß17.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] LoopDriver.runTurn executes the 10 PRD ¬ß10 steps in exact order: stabilize, deserialize core fragment, deserialize plugin fragments, diff, build prompt, decide, validate, act, notify-plugins, persist
- [ ] Per-turn 30s budget: an action that exceeds the wall-clock causes the turn to be marked failed and TurnTimeoutError captured in the trajectory
- [ ] Per-session budget: 50 turns OR 15 minutes wall-clock terminates the session cleanly with footer outcome=`budget_exhausted`
- [ ] Three consecutive failed turns terminates the session with footer outcome=`harness_error`, harnessError=`agent_stuck`
- [ ] Invalid action (validator reject) retries up to 3 times against the same observation with the rejection message spliced into the next prompt; the 4th attempt counts as a failed turn
- [ ] Malformed model output (SchemaRejection from provider) retries exactly once with the schema error injected; 2nd schema failure counts as a failed turn
- [ ] Plugin throwing during observation deserialize, busy-state, or onActionExecuted is logged but the turn proceeds without that plugin's contribution
- [ ] After exactly 3 consecutive failures from a single plugin, the driver calls `session.disablePlugin(namespace, reason)`; subsequent turns omit that plugin's tools from the merged list and skip its observation fragment; other plugins are unaffected
- [ ] A successful plugin observation resets that plugin's consecutive-failure counter to zero
- [ ] VM service connection lost (transport exception from VmServiceClient) terminates the session with footer outcome=`harness_error`, harnessError=`connection_lost`; trajectory header + all completed turns + footer are present on disk
- [ ] BindingNotInitializedError surfaces unwrapped to the caller of `session.start()`; no trajectory is written
- [ ] Voluntary `core.done(reason)` action, after successful execution, terminates the session with footer outcome=`done` and finalSummary capturing the reason
- [ ] Plugin auto-disable emits a `plugin_disabled` trajectory record (between turns) with namespace, reason, and turn index
- [ ] After every turn (including failed turns), the trajectory writer is flushed before the next turn begins
- [ ] Stability budget expired with plugins still busy is captured in `observation.stability` and is NOT a failed turn

title: Action validator against merged tool list
description: Given a candidate action and the current observation, validate: tool name exists in merged tool list (host core + active plugin tools); args match the tool's JsonSchema; for core tools that target semantic node IDs, the node exists, is enabled, and is visible. On rejection, return a structured failure designed to be model-readable. Driver retries the same observation up to 3 times. PRD ¬ß10 step 7, ¬ß17.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ActionValidator.validate(action, observation, tools)` returns `ValidationOk` for any well-formed candidate that passes all three passes
- [ ] Returns `ValidationReject` with `reason: 'unknown_tool'` when `action.tool` is not present in `tools`, populating `expected` with the available tool names
- [ ] Returns `ValidationReject` with `reason: 'schema_invalid'` when `action.args` fails the tool's `inputSchema` (json_schema draft-07), populating `pointer` with the JSON Pointer of the bad field and `description` with a one-line human description
- [ ] For core tools `core.tap`, `core.long_press`, `core.enter_text`, `core.scroll_until_visible`, `core.inspect_widget`: returns `ValidationReject` with `reason: 'node_not_found'` when the referenced `node_id` is not in `observation.core.nodes`
- [ ] For the same core tools: returns `ValidationReject` with `reason: 'node_disabled'` when the node exists but `enabled == false`
- [ ] For the same core tools: returns `ValidationReject` with `reason: 'node_not_visible'` when the node exists and is enabled but `visible == false`
- [ ] For `core.scroll_until_visible`, both `scrollable_id` and `target_id` are checked ‚Äî rejecting if either node fails the existence/enabled/visible passes
- [ ] Plugin-namespaced tool names (anything not in the `core.` namespace) skip the semantic-node pass ‚Äî only schema validation applies
- [ ] `ValidationReject.toModelMessage()` returns a single structured JSON string `{tool, reason, expected?, got?, pointer?, description?}` suitable for splicing into a retry prompt
- [ ] Validator is stateless: same `(action, observation, tools)` produces the same `ValidationResult` across repeated calls
- [ ] Validator imports no `dart:io` and the existing `tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` guard still passes

title: swift-infer model provider (local MLX gateway, Anthropic-compat)
description:
The local-inference provider for the harness. Targets the user's swift-infer server ‚Äî a Swift-based MLX gateway that exposes Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (and other MLX-served models) via Anthropic-compatible /v1/messages and OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions HTTP. v0.5 PRD ¬ß16.3's 'MLX provider' = swift-infer in this architecture; the abstract 'mlx-vlm/SGLang fallback' paths from earlier drafts are off the table.

Wire protocol: Anthropic-compat. Reuses .36's helpers (FrontierDefaults, VisionImage, validateToolArgs, lookupTool) ‚Äî .15 is essentially '.36 with a swift-infer base URL plus a couple of swift-infer-specific knobs.'

Vision capability gating: swift-infer's VLM support is being added in parallel by the user. Until it lands, this provider MUST report ModelCapabilities.vision = false so the harness loop (.18) skips screenshot inclusion. Default to vision=false in v1 ship. Flip via a config flag once swift-infer's VLM endpoint is verified working end-to-end. AC must include both states: 'with vision=false the harness completes a turn without screenshots' AND 'with vision=true a vision-bearing turn round-trips correctly.'

Sampling defaults per PRD ¬ß16.3 for Qwen3.6: temperature=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=20, presence_penalty=1.5, repetition_penalty=1.0, preserve_thinking=true. ThinkingDelta stream from .14 must surface Qwen3.6's <think>...</think> reasoning to the DevTools panel (success criterion ¬ß23 #7).

Cross-refs: PRD ¬ß16.1, ¬ß16.3 (primary target). Local-only ‚Äî no critical path through frontier providers.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider` class is defined in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart` and implements `ModelProvider` from `.14`; `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` reports `No issues found!`
- [ ] `SwiftInferConfig` value class exposes `Uri baseUrl`, `String model`, `String? apiKey`, `bool enableVision = false`, and overridable sampling fields `temperature`, `topP`, `topK`, `presencePenalty`, `repetitionPenalty`, `preserveThinking`, with the ¬ß16.3 Qwen3.6 defaults (`temperature=1.0`, `topP=0.95`, `topK=20`, `presencePenalty=1.5`, `repetitionPenalty=1.0`, `preserveThinking=true`)
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider.decide(PromptPayload, ActionSchema)` issues a POST to `<config.baseUrl>/v1/messages` with header `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`, optional `x-api-key: <apiKey>` only when set, JSON body whose top-level fields include `model`, `max_tokens`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`, `presence_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`, `preserve_thinking`, `system`, `tools`, and `messages`, and parses the first `tool_use` content block into a `ModelDecision` whose `action.tool` matches the dotted tool name and whose `action.args` is the block's `input` map
- [ ] When `enableVision == false`, `capabilities.vision` is `false`; the request body's `messages[].content` MUST NOT contain any block whose `type == 'image'`, even when `PromptPayload.userMessages` was constructed with one (the provider strips image blocks before send so the loop driver in `.18` can stay capability-agnostic)
- [ ] When `enableVision == true`, `capabilities.vision` is `true`; image blocks built by `VisionImage.toAnthropicBlock()` are forwarded unchanged in `messages[].content`
- [ ] When the response contains zero `tool_use` blocks, `decide()` throws `SchemaRejection` whose `validationError` reads `no tool_use block in response` and `rawOutput` contains the response body
- [ ] When the parsed `tool_use` block produces a JSON envelope that fails `ActionSchema.validate()`, `decide()` re-throws the `SchemaRejection` raised by `ActionSchema.validate()` unchanged (no swallow, no retry inside the provider)
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider.thinking()` returns a `Stream<ThinkingDelta>` that is fed by an SSE decoder of the `/v1/messages` streaming endpoint (`stream: true`); each `<think>...</think>` chunk arriving in `content_block_delta` events of type `text_delta` is surfaced as a `ThinkingDelta(text: chunk, isFinal: false)`, and the closing `</think>` boundary emits `ThinkingDelta(text: '', isFinal: true)`
- [ ] `capabilities` reports `preserveThinking: true`, `supportsToolUse: true`, `maxContext: 128000`, and `vision` mirrors `config.enableVision`
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider` and `SwiftInferConfig` are exported from `package:exploration_agent` via the provider barrel so `.18` and `.20` can construct it without reaching into `src/`
- [ ] Mock-client unit tests cover: happy-path tool_use decode, sampling parameters appear in request body with ¬ß16.3 defaults, vision-off request omits image blocks, vision-on request includes image block with base64 PNG payload, missing-tool_use throws `SchemaRejection`, `ActionSchema` rejection escapes unchanged, thinking-stream emits `ThinkingDelta` chunks as SSE events arrive, `apiKey` header is omitted when null
- [ ] Package `exploration_agent` remains web-compatible: `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` exits 0 after this bead's sources are added
notes:
## Cross-references

- PRD ¬ß16.1 ‚Äî provider abstraction + dynamic schema (consumed via .14)
- PRD ¬ß16.3 ‚Äî Qwen3.6-35B-A3B sampling: temperature=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=20, presence_penalty=1.5, repetition_penalty=1.0, preserve_thinking=true
- PRD ¬ß22 ‚Äî web-compat (no dart:io; package:http); CORS risk for browser-hosted DevTools panel
- PRD ¬ß23 #7 ‚Äî success criterion: thinking-trace stream keeps up with model gen rate (SSE-driven thinking() emits per-chunk)
- Sibling .14 ‚Äî defines ModelProvider, ActionSchema, value types, SchemaRejection
- Sibling .36 ‚Äî defines VisionImage, lookupTool, encodeToolName, validateToolArgs (this provider reuses them; FrontierDefaults intentionally NOT reused since Qwen sampling differs)
- Critical-path note: this provider is the v1 default for the loop driver .18; .36 is the frontier fallback when local stalls per PRD ¬ß16.4

## Deployment / operator notes (out of scope for this bead)

- swift-infer must serve CORS headers when the harness runs inside the DevTools extension (browser context). At minimum:
  - `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <devtools-origin>` (or `*` for dev)
  - `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: content-type, x-api-key, anthropic-version`
  - `Access-Control-Allow-Methods: POST, OPTIONS`
- VLM (vision) endpoint readiness in swift-infer is tracked in the user's `../swift-infer` repo; flip `SwiftInferConfig.enableVision = true` only after end-to-end VLM verification.
- Spike .18f (mlx-vlm tool-call parser) was closed as obviated ‚Äî the swift-infer architecture removed the question entirely. Do not re-introduce a dep on .18f.

## Architecture decision recorded

- Wire protocol: Anthropic-compat (`/v1/messages`) over OpenAI-compat (`/v1/chat/completions`). Single decision; future bead may add an `SwiftInferConfig.protocol` switch if needed.

title: Model provider abstraction with dynamic constrained-output schema
description: ModelProvider interface with completion + schema-constrained variants. Schema is dynamically composed each turn from the current merged tool list ‚Äî different plugins yield different valid action shapes. Output shape: {action: {tool, args}, summary_update, rationale?, wait_strategy?}. Outputs that don't match the schema are rejected and re-prompted once. Surfaces model capabilities (vision, preserve_thinking, max context) so the host can default screenshot capture appropriately. PRD ¬ß16.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ModelProvider` interface defined in `exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/model_provider.dart` with `capabilities`, `thinking()`, and `decide(PromptPayload, ActionSchema)` members; `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` passes
- [ ] `ModelCapabilities` class exposes typed fields `vision: bool`, `preserveThinking: bool`, `maxContext: int`, `supportsToolUse: bool`
- [ ] `ModelDecision` class exposes typed fields `action: {tool: String, args: Map<String, dynamic>}`, `summaryUpdate: String?`, `rationale: String?`, `waitStrategy: String?`
- [ ] `ActionSchema.fromToolList(List<ToolDescriptor>)` produces a JSON Schema (draft-07) whose `action` field is a `oneOf` discriminated union over each tool's `name` + `inputSchema`
- [ ] Schema composed from a 2-tool list differs (deep-equality) from schema composed from a 1-tool list (test asserts JSON inequality)
- [ ] Schema is recomposed on every call ‚Äî no memoization across calls (test asserts identical input lists yield equal but distinct map instances)
- [ ] `SchemaRejection` exception type carries `validationError: String` and `rawOutput: String`; thrown by `ActionSchema.validate()` when output fails JSON-Schema validation
- [ ] `ModelProvider` interface contract documents the one-retry-on-rejection rule (the loop driver enforces; provider surface only throws `SchemaRejection`)
- [ ] `ModelProvider.thinking()` returns a `Stream<ThinkingDelta>` where `ThinkingDelta` carries `text: String` and `isFinal: bool`
- [ ] Package `exploration_agent` remains web-compatible: no transitive `dart:io` import ‚Äî verified via `dart pub deps --json` parse asserting no `dart:io` symbol in the import graph for the new files
- [ ] All unit tests under `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/` pass via `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/`

title: Observation assembly & per-turn structural diff (core + plugins)
description: Pull observation bundle via getStableObservation, normalize, compute per-turn structural diff for both core fragment and each plugin fragment. Plugins owning a delta-friendly shape get a structured diff; opaque blobs fall back to a previous-vs-current pair. Diffs are harness-authored and included verbatim in the next prompt. PRD ¬ß10, ¬ß11.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ExplorationSession.observe()` issues a single VM call to `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation` and deserializes the response into a typed `Observation { core: CoreFragment, plugins: Map<String, PluginFragment>, stability: StabilityMetadata }`
- [ ] `Observation.empty()` constructs a zero-content prior with no nodes, no routes, no errors, and no plugin keys
- [ ] `ObservationDiffer.diff(prev, curr)` returns a typed `ObservationDiff { core: CoreDiff, plugins: Map<String, PluginDiff> }`
- [ ] `CoreDiff` exposes `routeChanges`, `nodesAdded`, `nodesRemoved`, `nodesChanged`, `errorsAdded`
- [ ] First-turn diff (prev = `Observation.empty()`) returns `nodesAdded` covering all current nodes, `nodesRemoved` empty, and no exceptions
- [ ] Plugin fragments declared `deltaFriendly` at registration produce a `PluginDiff.structured` with key-level `added`, `removed`, `changed` maps
- [ ] Plugin fragments not declared delta-friendly produce a `PluginDiff.opaque` with `previous` and `current` JSON values
- [ ] A plugin namespace present in `curr` but absent in `prev` is reported as a `PluginDiff.added` entry without exception
- [ ] A plugin namespace present in `prev` but absent in `curr` is reported as a `PluginDiff.removed` entry without exception
- [ ] Diff output is deterministic: calling `diff(a, b)` twice on identical inputs yields byte-identical JSON serialization
- [ ] Diff result is JSON-serializable (round-trips via `toJson`/`fromJson`) for inclusion in the next prompt and trajectory
- [ ] No transitive `dart:io` import is introduced by either module (verified by the existing CI guard from cx6.11)

title: Harness scaffolding, VM service connection, and ExplorationSession API
description: exploration_agent library shell that connects to a running app's VM service URI, performs the binding handshake, and exposes a public ExplorationSession API the two frontends consume. Web-compatible by construction (no dart:io anywhere in this package). Provides typed VmServiceClient abstraction reused by later harness stories. PRD ¬ß6.2.
design:
## Approach
Stand up `exploration_agent` as a pure Dart library (no executable entrypoint) whose public surface is the `ExplorationSession` class. v0.5 refactored the harness/frontend boundary so this library is consumed by both `exploration_cli` (CLI frontend) and `exploration_devtools` (DevTools extension panel) ‚Äî same loop, two surfaces, no reimplementation.

`ExplorationSession` owns the lifecycle: `start(goal, config)`, `observe()`, `act(action)`, `end()`, plus a stream-based progress channel for the DevTools thinking panel. Internally it composes a typed `VmServiceClient` abstraction that wraps `package:vm_service` for the calls the harness needs (binding handshake, `getStableObservation`, `executeAction`, namespaced extension dispatch). This client is the shared seam reused by .12 (observation pull), .14 (model provider state), .17 (validator), and .19 (writer).

Handshake protocol: connect to VM service URI, look up `ext.flutter.exploration.handshake`, exchange contract-version + active-plugin manifest. Absence of the binding extension ‚Üí typed `BindingNotInitializedError` whose message points at the host setup snippet from PRD ¬ß7.6.

## Constraints
- **MUST be web-compatible** per PRD ¬ß22: no `dart:io`, no transitive imports of it. CI guard verifies the package's transitive dep graph contains no `dart:io` symbols. Trajectory I/O is delegated to .19's writer abstraction; the harness library itself never touches the filesystem directly.
- Public API is the only commitment surface ‚Äî internals are private and rearrangeable in later stories.
- Single binding per process (the host enforces this in .2); we can assume exactly one ExplorationBinding on the other end.

## Ruled out
- Harness as a CLI executable (v0.4 shape): broken by v0.5 because two frontends now consume the same loop. A library is the only shape that supports both without IPC duplication.
- Running the harness as a separate process from DevTools and IPC-ing into the panel: PRD ¬ß6.3 requires the loop run inside the extension web app itself; that forecloses any process-boundary design.
- Hand-rolled VM service JSON-RPC: package:vm_service is the supported Dart abstraction.

## Non-obvious notes
- Blocks .12, .13, .14, .17, .19, .21, .25, .26, .27 ‚Äî this is the harness anchor. Land its public API first; later stories implement against the surface.
- The `VmServiceClient` typed abstraction is load-bearing across phase 2; spending design weight on its shape here saves churn in .12/.17/.19.
- Cross-references: PRD ¬ß6.2 (library refactor), ¬ß6.3 (in-panel loop requirement), ¬ß22 (web-compat constraint), ¬ß7.6 (binding handshake message shape).
- Plugin auto-disable state lives on the session (consumed by .18); expose a minimal mutator now so .18 doesn't need to reach into private state.


## Implementation Plan

1. Add deps to `packages/exploration_agent/pubspec.yaml`

   Set `name: exploration_agent`, SDK `>=3.4.0 <4.0.0`. `dependencies: vm_service: ^14.2.0, meta: ^1.12.0`. `dev_dependencies: test: ^1.25.0, lints: ^4.0.0`.

   Test: `dart pub get -C packages/exploration_agent` ‚Üí `Got dependencies!`
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): add vm_service dependency`

2. Define `BindingNotInitializedError` ‚Äî `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/errors.dart`

   ```dart
   class BindingNotInitializedError extends StateError {
     BindingNotInitializedError() : super(_msg);
     static const _msg = '''
   ExplorationBinding is not initialized in the target app.
   Add to your app's main():

     void main() {
       if (kDebugMode) {
         ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [/* your plugins */]);
       } else {
         WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();
       }
       runApp(MyApp());
     }
   ''';
   }
   ```

   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/errors_test.dart` ‚Üí PASS
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): add BindingNotInitializedError`

3. Define DTOs and progress events ‚Äî `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/types.dart`

   Concrete classes (all with `const` constructors and `final` fields):

   - `HandshakeResult({required String contractVersion, required List<PluginManifestEntry> plugins})`
   - `PluginManifestEntry({required String namespace, required List<String> tools})` ‚Äî `tools` are pre-namespaced (e.g. `router.go`)
   - `ExplorationConfig({Duration turnBudget = 30s, Duration sessionBudget = 15min, int maxTurns = 50})`
   - `sealed class SessionProgressEvent { const SessionProgressEvent(); }`
   - `class SessionStarted(this.goal) extends SessionProgressEvent` ‚Äî field `final String goal`
   - `class TurnBegan(this.turn) extends SessionProgressEvent` ‚Äî field `final int turn`
   - `class PluginAutoDisabled(this.namespace, this.reason) extends SessionProgressEvent` ‚Äî fields `final String namespace, reason`
   - `class SessionEnded extends SessionProgressEvent { const SessionEnded(); }`

   Test: `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/types.dart` ‚Üí `No issues found!`
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): add session DTOs and progress events`

4. Implement typed `VmServiceClient` ‚Äî `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart`

   Single class wrapping `package:vm_service`. Private constructor `VmServiceClient._(VmService _vm, String _isolateId)`. Public surface:

   ```dart
   static Future<VmServiceClient> connect(Uri wsUri);
   Future<HandshakeResult> handshake();
   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> getStableObservation(Map<String, dynamic> policy);
   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> executeAction(String name, Map<String, dynamic> args);
   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> callExtension(String extension, Map<String, dynamic> args);
   Future<void> dispose();
   ```

   Implementation rules:
   - `connect`: `vmServiceConnectUri(wsUri.toString())` from `package:vm_service/vm_service_io.dart`, then `await vm.getVM()`, take `state.isolates!.first.id!`.
   - `handshake`: calls `_safeCall('ext.flutter.exploration.handshake', {})`. Decode `{contractVersion: String, plugins: [{namespace: String, tools: [String]}]}` into `HandshakeResult`.
   - `getStableObservation` ‚Üí `_safeCall('ext.flutter.exploration.core.getStableObservation', policy)`.
   - `executeAction(name, args)` ‚Üí `_safeCall('ext.flutter.exploration.core.executeAction', {'name': name, 'args': args})`.
   - `callExtension` ‚Üí generic escape hatch passing the literal extension name through (consumed by .12 for plugin extensions).

5. Implement `ExplorationSession` ‚Äî `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart`

   Class fields: `final VmServiceClient _client`, `final StreamController<SessionProgressEvent> _progress = StreamController.broadcast()`, `final Set<String> _disabled = {}`, `HandshakeResult? _handshake`, `bool _started = false`. Public surface:

   ```dart
   static Future<ExplorationSession> connect(Uri vmServiceUri);
   Stream<SessionProgressEvent> get progress;
   Set<String> get disabledPlugins;          // unmodifiable
   HandshakeResult get handshake;            // throws if !_started
   Future<void> start(String goal, ExplorationConfig config);
   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> observe();
   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> act(Map<String, dynamic> action);
   void disablePlugin(String namespace, String reason);
   Future<void> end();
   ```

   Behaviour rules:
   - `connect(uri)` ‚Üí `ExplorationSession._(await VmServiceClient.connect(uri))`.
   - `start`: throw `StateError('Session already started')` if `_started`; await `_client.handshake()`, store on `_handshake`, set `_started = true`, emit `SessionStarted(goal)`.
   - `observe()` and `act()`: call `_ensureStarted()` (throws `StateError('start() must complete before observe()/act()')` if `!_started`); delegate to `_client.getStableObservation(const {})` and `_client.executeAction(action['name'] as String, (action['args'] as Map).cast())` respectively.
   - `disablePlugin(ns, reason)`: `_disabled.add(ns)`; emit `PluginAutoDisabled(ns, reason)`.
   - `end`: no-op if `!_started`; emit `SessionEnded`; `await _progress.close(); await _client.dispose();`.
   - Add `@visibleForTesting` factory `ExplorationSession.forTest(VmServiceClient client)`.

   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/session_test.dart` ‚Üí PASS (start emits SessionStarted; double-start throws; disablePlugin records+emits; observe/act before start throw StateError)
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): ExplorationSession lifecycle and progress channel`

   ```dart
   // tool/check_no_dart_io.dart
   import 'dart:io';
   void main() {
     final hits = <String>[];
     final re = RegExp(r"""import\s+['"]dart:io['"]""");
     for (final f in Directory('packages/exploration_agent/lib').listSync(recursive: true).whereType<File>()) {
       if (f.path.endsWith('.dart') && re.hasMatch(f.readAsStringSync())) hits.add(f.path);
     }
     if (hits.isNotEmpty) {

     }

   }
   ```

title: Stable observation primitive (getStableObservation)
description: Binding extension that polls framework signals and busyState() from all plugins until the chosen policy terminates, then captures and returns the merged observation: core fragment (semantics, routes-best-effort, errors, stability metadata, optional screenshot) + per-plugin fragments under observation.plugins.<namespace>. Implements three policies: action-relative (default 800ms budget), quiet-frame (default N=2), bounded-stability (default 1500ms budget). Per-plugin observation budget enforcement (default 1KB serialized) with warning + truncation on overrun. Stability metadata reports terminated_by, duration_ms, framework_busy, plugins_busy[]. PRD ¬ß9, ¬ß10, ¬ß11.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] VM service extension `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation` registered exactly once during binding init; returns a single JSON object (no streaming, no follow-ups)
- [ ] Request accepts `policy` ‚àà {`action-relative`, `quiet-frame`, `bounded-stability`}; default is `action-relative`; unknown policy returns a structured error
- [ ] Request accepts optional overrides: `actionRelativeBudgetMs` (default 800), `quietFrameN` (default 2), `boundedStabilityBudgetMs` (default 1500); overrides above 30s are clamped and a warning is logged
- [ ] action-relative terminates on first of: route stack change OR semantics tree change OR `frameworkBusySnapshot()` reports idle AND every plugin `busyState().isBusy == false` OR `actionRelativeBudgetMs` elapses
- [ ] quiet-frame terminates after `quietFrameN` consecutive committed frames where framework signals are idle AND every plugin reports not busy (no wall-clock cap on this policy itself)
- [ ] bounded-stability terminates on quiet-frame condition OR `boundedStabilityBudgetMs` wall-clock; on budget expiration the response still captures and `stability.terminated_by == "budget"` with `plugins_busy[]` and `framework_busy` populated
- [ ] Response contains `stability` with `policy`, `terminated_by` ‚àà {`route_change`, `semantics_change`, `idle`, `quiet_frame`, `budget`}, `duration_ms`, `framework_busy` (verbatim from cx6.4 snapshot), and `plugins_busy[]` (each `{namespace, reason, est_ms}` from cx6.3 BusyState)
- [ ] Core fragment is composed from cx6.5 semantics, best-effort Navigator 1.0 route stack, cx6.9 errors-since-cursor, optional cx6.7 screenshot when caller passes `includeScreenshot=true`, and stability metadata; total core serialized size budget defaults to 4096 bytes
- [ ] Per-plugin fragments are placed under `observation.plugins.<namespace>` in plugin registration order; default per-plugin budget is 1024 bytes serialized, overridable via the optional `pluginBudgets` map on the request
- [ ] Plugin fragment that exceeds its budget is truncated and replaced with `{ "_truncated": true, "originalBytes": <int>, "budgetBytes": <int> }`; a warning is logged via `developer.log` with the plugin namespace
- [ ] Sum of plugin fragment budgets is capped at 2048 bytes; if `pluginBudgets` exceeds this cap, each plugin's effective budget is scaled down proportionally and a warning is logged
- [ ] Plugin `busyState()` or `observe()` that throws does NOT abort the call ‚Äî the plugin contributes "not busy" / no fragment for that turn and increments its strike counter via the cx6.3 registry contract
- [ ] kDebugMode-gated: in release/profile-only builds the extension is not registered (mirrors cx6.2 binding gating)
- [ ] Unit tests cover: each policy's termination conditions, budget overrun truncation, plugin throw isolation, override clamping, and the `terminated_by` enumeration

title: Core action tools (core namespace)
description: Implement and register the host's 10 core tools under the 'core' namespace: core.tap, core.long_press, core.enter_text, core.scroll, core.scroll_until_visible, core.gesture (discrete kinds), core.system_back, core.wait (rejected if >5s), core.inspect_widget, core.done. All target semantics node IDs except wait/done. PRD ¬ß12.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 10 core tools (`tap`, `long_press`, `enter_text`, `scroll`, `scroll_until_visible`, `gesture`, `system_back`, `wait`, `inspect_widget`, `done`) are registered under the `core` namespace via the plugin registry from cx6.3
- [ ] Registry rejects any non-host plugin attempting to claim the reserved `core` namespace with a clear error
- [ ] Each tool is reachable as a VM service extension at `ext.flutter.exploration.core.<tool>` and accepts/rejects payloads per its `inputSchema`
- [ ] Action tools (`tap`, `long_press`, `enter_text`, `scroll`, `scroll_until_visible`, `gesture`, `inspect_widget`) take a semantics node id as their target; calls referencing an unknown or disposed id return a structured `target_not_found` error
- [ ] `core.tap` and `core.long_press` dispatch via `SemanticsAction.tap` / `SemanticsAction.longPress` when the node advertises the action, and fall back to a synthesized hit-test pointer event at the node's center rect otherwise
- [ ] `core.enter_text` focuses the target (via `SemanticsAction.focus` or hit-test) before delivering text, and the target's resulting value reflects the requested string
- [ ] `core.scroll` accepts an axis and a signed pixel delta and dispatches `SemanticsAction.scrollUp/Down/Left/Right` (or pointer-drag fallback) on the target scrollable
- [ ] `core.scroll_until_visible` repeatedly scrolls the named scrollable until the target node appears in the semantics tree, capped at a configurable max iterations (default 20); on cap exhaustion it returns a `target_unreachable` error rather than looping
- [ ] `core.gesture` accepts only the discrete kinds `pan`, `swipe`, `pinch_in`, `pinch_out` with bounded numeric parameters; any other kind is rejected at schema validation
- [ ] `core.system_back` dispatches a back navigation via `SystemNavigator.pop` (or platform back-button equivalent through `ServicesBinding`) and returns success/failure
- [ ] `core.wait` rejects any `seconds` value `> 5.0` or `<= 0` at schema validation, and otherwise awaits the requested duration before returning
- [ ] `core.inspect_widget` returns a depth-capped (default 5 levels, ~4KB max) JSON dump of the widget subtree rooted at the target's nearest `Element`
- [ ] `core.done` accepts a `reason` string and emits a structured terminal record `{type: "done", reason}` that the loop driver (cx6.18) can read; subsequent action calls in the same session are rejected with `session_terminated`
- [ ] Unit tests cover every tool's success path, schema-rejection path, and target-not-found path against an instrumented test app

title: Semantics tree capture with stable IDs
description: Walk the semantics tree, assign session-stable numeric IDs, filter off-screen / fully-obscured / excludeSemantics nodes, and emit role/label/state-flags/available-actions per node. Expose as a host VM service extension. Apps with custom design systems whose controls don't expose semantics natively are addressed at the plugin layer (PRD ¬ß6.1: the host explicitly does not own custom-design-system knobs in v0.5; that surface is a plugin concern). PRD ¬ß11.1, ¬ß12.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `SemanticsCapture` walks the live `SemanticsOwner` rooted node tree and emits a `List<Map<String, dynamic>>` of node records on demand
- [ ] Each record contains exactly the keys: `id` (int), `role` (string), `label` (string, omitted if empty), `state` (list of string flags, omitted if empty), `actions` (list of string action names, omitted if empty), `rect` (4-int list `[l,t,r,b]` quantized to integer pixels)
- [ ] `id` is a session-stable numeric identifier: the same `SemanticsNode` (identified by its framework `SemanticsNode.id`) maps to the same emitted `id` across repeated captures within one binding lifetime; ids are not reused after a node is gc'd within the same session
- [ ] Off-screen nodes (bounding rect fully outside the device pixel ratio scaled `window.physicalSize` viewport) are omitted by default
- [ ] Nodes flagged `isHidden` or under an `excludeSemantics` boundary (i.e. absent from the merged tree because the framework excluded them) are omitted by default
- [ ] Fully obscured nodes (entirely covered by a later sibling/ancestor in paint order with the same or larger bounding rect) are omitted by default
- [ ] VM service extension `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_semantics` is registered by `ExplorationBinding` when semantics is enabled and returns a JSON envelope `{"type":"_extensionType","method":"...","semantics":[...records...],"count":N}`
- [ ] The extension calls `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` on first invocation so a host that has never opened a screen reader still produces a tree
- [ ] Unit test: capturing a known widget tree (one button, one off-screen text, one excludeSemantics subtree) returns exactly one record for the button with the expected role/label/actions
- [ ] Unit test: capturing the same tree twice yields the same `id` for the persistent node
- [ ] All public symbols are documented and `dart analyze` passes with zero warnings on `exploration_flutter`

title: Frame stability signal collection (host)
description: Hook SchedulerBinding to track transient callbacks (animations), persistent callbacks, pending microtasks, frame commit/skip events. Maintain bounded counters and timestamps suitable for stability decisions. Used by getStableObservation. PRD ¬ß6.1, ¬ß9.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `FrameworkBusySnapshot` is a public immutable value type in `exploration_flutter` with fields: `transientCallbacks` (int), `persistentCallbacks` (int), `pendingMicrotasks` (bool), `lastFrameCommitTimestamp` (Duration?), `recentSkippedFrames` (int), and `recentFrameCommits` (List<Duration>, bounded length).
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding.frameworkBusySnapshot()` returns a `FrameworkBusySnapshot` synchronously without allocating new collections beyond the returned record (verified by an allocation-bounded test that calls it 1000x in a tight loop and asserts the bounded ring buffer length never exceeds the documented cap).
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding.isAnyFrameworkSignalBusy` getter returns `true` iff any of: `transientCallbacks > 0`, `persistentCallbacks > 0`, or `pendingMicrotasks == true`.
- [ ] `transientCallbacks` count increments when `SchedulerBinding.scheduleFrameCallback` is invoked and decrements when the callback fires (verified by a test that schedules a transient callback, observes count==1, pumps a frame, observes count==0).
- [ ] `persistentCallbacks` count reflects registrations via `SchedulerBinding.addPersistentFrameCallback` (verified by a test that registers two persistent callbacks and observes count==2).
- [ ] `pendingMicrotasks` reads true after a microtask is scheduled and before the microtask queue drains for the current event-loop turn (verified via `scheduleMicrotask` + immediate-read test). Documented as a best-effort edge signal.
- [ ] `lastFrameCommitTimestamp` is set after each `SchedulerPhase.postFrameCallbacks` completion to the frame's `currentFrameTimeStamp` (verified by pumping a frame and asserting the timestamp matches `binding.currentFrameTimeStamp`).
- [ ] `recentFrameCommits` is a bounded ring buffer (cap = 16) of the most recent commit timestamps; oldest entries evict on overflow (verified by pumping 20 frames and asserting `length == 16`).
- [ ] `recentSkippedFrames` increments when `SchedulerBinding.handleBeginFrame` reports a skip (frame interval > expected `vsync` budget, threshold 2x); resets to 0 after `recentFrameCommits` cap full of non-skip frames (verified by a forced long-frame test using `WidgetTester` time control).
- [ ] All counters are bounded ‚Äî calling the snapshot 10,000 times across a long-running session does not cause memory growth (verified via Dart VM heap snapshot before/after, asserting < 10KB delta attributable to the binding's stability fields).
- [ ] Functionality is gated to `kDebugMode || kProfileMode`; in release mode `frameworkBusySnapshot()` returns a zero-valued snapshot and the scheduler hooks are not installed (verified by a build-mode-conditional test).
- [ ] Field names in `FrameworkBusySnapshot` align with PRD ¬ß9.2's `framework_busy` block (`transient_callbacks`, `microtasks`) such that cx6.8's serializer does not need translation (verified by a test that JSON-encodes the snapshot and asserts keys match `transient_callbacks`, `persistent_callbacks`, `microtasks`, `last_frame_commit_us`, `recent_skipped_frames`).

title: Plugin contract: types, registry, and PluginContext
description: The load-bearing v0.5 design artifact. Define ExplorationPlugin, ExplorationTool, BusyState, ObservationContext, ExecutedAction, ToolResult, JsonSchema. Implement the plugin registry: registration order preserved, namespace uniqueness enforced (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$), tool names mandatorily prefixed (<namespace>.<tool>), collision rejection at startup. Implement PluginContext with registerErrorHandler, registerExtension (namespaced under ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.<suffix>), registerFrameCallback. Lifecycle: initialize() in registration order, dispose() at session end. Per-method exception isolation; auto-disable a plugin after 3 consecutive failures. PRD ¬ß7.
design:
## Approach
Implement the v0.5 plugin contract (PRD ¬ß7.1‚Äì¬ß7.8) in `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/`: `ExplorationPlugin`/`ExplorationTool` interfaces, value types, `PluginContext` with auto-namespaced VM extensions, and a `PluginRegistry` enforcing `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` namespaces, mandatory `<ns>.<tool>` prefixing, registration-order preservation, per-method exception isolation, and per-(plugin √ó method) 3-strikes auto-disable. `initialize()` failures are non-fatal. Source carries doc comments capturing PRD ¬ß7.7 versioning posture and PRD ¬ß8 v2 extension points.

## Constraints
- v1 contract marked EXPERIMENTAL in source. cx6.8/.9/.17/.25/.26/.27/.35 consume these names verbatim.
- `_entries` is a `List` so registration order survives every dispatch (PRD ¬ß7.8).
- Auto-disable is per-(plugin √ó method); `initialize` failure flips `initFailed` once and short-circuits all later calls (PRD ¬ß17).
- Auto-disable log line emitted exactly once per (plugin, method): `[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>`.

## Ruled out
- Auto-discovery from `pubspec.yaml` (PRD ¬ß18); plugin-to-plugin hard deps (PRD ¬ß21); subclassing the binding for extensibility (PRD ¬ß7.5); standalone error-chaining bead (collapsed into `registerErrorHandler`).

## Implementation Plan

1. Value types ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/types.dart`

   Five `const`-constructible classes with final fields:
   - `JsonSchema(Map<String,Object?> raw)`
   - `ToolResult({required bool ok, Object? value, String? error})`
   - `BusyState({required bool isBusy, String? reason, Duration? estimatedDuration})` + `static const idle = BusyState(isBusy:false)`
   - `ObservationContext({required int turn, required Duration sinceLastAction})`
   - `ExecutedAction({required String toolName, required Map<String,Object?> args, required ToolResult result})` (toolName fully qualified)

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/types.dart` ‚Üí clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): value types"`

2. Plugin & tool interfaces ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart`

   Imports `types.dart`, `plugin_context.dart`. Two abstract classes:
   - `ExplorationTool` ‚Äî getters `String name` (bare token), `String description`, `JsonSchema inputSchema`; method `Future<ToolResult> call(Map<String, Object?> args)`.
   - `ExplorationPlugin` ‚Äî getters `String namespace` (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`), `List<ExplorationTool> tools`; methods `Future<void> initialize(PluginContext)`, `Future<Map<String, Object?>?> observe(ObservationContext)`, `Future<BusyState> busyState()`, `Future<void> onActionExecuted(ExecutedAction)`, `Future<void> dispose()`.

   Class-level dartdoc on `ExplorationPlugin` MUST contain (verbatim ‚Äî validation greps for these phrases): `EXPERIMENTAL ‚Äî v1 plugin contract (PRD ¬ß7).`, `Versioning (PRD ¬ß7.7): adding tools, fragment fields, or busy heuristics is NOT breaking. Unknown fragment fields are passed through opaquely.`, `v2 extension (PRD ¬ß8): List<ExplorationResource> get resources and List<ExplorationPrompt> get prompts may be added with default empty implementations without breaking existing plugins.`

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` ‚Üí clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): ExplorationPlugin and ExplorationTool with v2 docs"`

3. PluginContext ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart`

   Imports `dart:developer as developer`, `package:flutter/foundation.dart`, `package:flutter/scheduler.dart`.
   - `typedef ErrorHandler = bool Function(FlutterErrorDetails);`
   - `typedef ExtensionHandler = Future<developer.ServiceExtensionResponse> Function(String method, Map<String, String> parameters);`
   - `class PluginContext` ‚Äî fields `final String namespace`, `final SchedulerBinding _scheduler`, `final List<ErrorHandler> errorHandlers = []`. Ctor `PluginContext({required this.namespace, required SchedulerBinding scheduler}) : _scheduler = scheduler;`. Static `@visibleForTesting String buildExtensionMethodName(String ns, String suffix) => 'ext.flutter.exploration.$ns.$suffix';`. Methods: `registerErrorHandler` appends to `errorHandlers`; `registerExtension(suffix, h)` calls `developer.registerExtension(buildExtensionMethodName(namespace, suffix), h)`; `registerFrameCallback(cb)` calls `_scheduler.addPostFrameCallback(cb)`.

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart` ‚Üí clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): PluginContext with auto-namespaced VM extensions"`

4. PluginRegistry ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart`

   Internals: `enum _Method { observe, busyState, onActionExecuted }`; per-plugin `_Entry { plugin; context; bool initFailed=false; Map<_Method,int> failures; Map<_Method,bool> disabled; }` (failure counters init 0, disabled init false for every `_Method`). State: `List<_Entry> _entries = []`, `bool _finalized = false`, `_nsRe = RegExp(r'^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$')`. Ctor `PluginRegistry({required SchedulerBinding scheduler})`.

   Public API:
   - `register(ExplorationPlugin p)` ‚Äî throws `StateError` if `_finalized`; `ArgumentError.value(p.namespace, 'namespace', 'must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\$')` on regex failure; `StateError('duplicate plugin namespace: ${p.namespace}')` on duplicate; else appends `_Entry(p, PluginContext(namespace: p.namespace, scheduler: _scheduler))`.
   - `mergedTools()` (alias `finalize()`) ‚Äî for each entry/tool: throw `ArgumentError.value(t.name, 'tool.name', 'must be bare token (no ".")')` on dotted name; `fq = '${ns}.${t.name}'`; throw `StateError('tool name collision: $fq')` on duplicate; insert in registration order. Set `_finalized = true`; return `Map.unmodifiable(out)`.
   - `initializeAll()` ‚Äî sequential per-plugin try/catch; on throw set `e.initFailed = true` and `debugPrint('[exploration] plugin ${e.plugin.namespace} initialize failed: $err\n$st')`; never rethrows.
   - `_guard<R>(_Entry e, _Method m, Future<R> Function() body, R fallback)` ‚Äî return `fallback` if `e.initFailed || e.disabled[m]!`. Else `try` body, reset `failures[m]=0` on success. On throw increment, `debugPrint('... ${m.name} threw ...')`, and on first transition to `>=3` set `disabled[m]=true` and emit one `'[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>'`; return `fallback`.
   - `observeAll(ctx)` ‚Äî guarded, fallback `null`; inserts only non-null fragments under `e.plugin.namespace` in registration order.
   - `busyStateAll()` ‚Äî guarded, fallback `BusyState.idle`; returns `List<MapEntry<String, BusyState>>`.
   - `onActionExecutedAll(a)` ‚Äî guarded, fallback `null`.
   - `disposeAll()` ‚Äî sequential, per-plugin try/catch; runs every plugin even if earlier ones throw; `debugPrint`s on throw.
   - `dispatchError(FlutterErrorDetails d)` ‚Äî outer entries (skip `initFailed`); inner `e.context.errorHandlers` in order; first returning `true` short-circuits ‚Üí `true`; per-handler exceptions are `debugPrint`ed and treated as not-claiming; returns `false` if none claimed.

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart` ‚Üí clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): PluginRegistry with validation, prefixing, isolation, 3-strikes"`

5. Public barrel ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart`

   ```dart
   export 'src/contract/types.dart';
   export 'src/contract/plugin.dart';
   export 'src/contract/plugin_context.dart';
   export 'src/contract/registry.dart' show PluginRegistry;
   ```

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` ‚Üí clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): public barrel"`

6. Registry tests ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/test/contract/registry_test.dart`

   In-file helper fakes:
   - `_FakeTool` ‚Äî `name` captured; `description=''`; `inputSchema=const JsonSchema({})`; `call` returns `const ToolResult(ok:true)`.
   - `_OrderPlugin({namespace, log, toolNames=const []})` ‚Äî appends `'init:$ns'/'obs:$ns'/'busy:$ns'/'act:$ns'/'dispose:$ns'` to `log`; `observe` returns `{'k':1, 'unknown_future_field':'opaque'}`.
   - `_ThrowOnObserve` extends `_OrderPlugin` ‚Äî `observe` logs then throws `StateError('boom')`.
   - `_ThrowOnDispose` extends `_OrderPlugin` ‚Äî `dispose` logs then throws.

   Setup in `main()`: `TestWidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized(); final scheduler = SchedulerBinding.instance;`. Each test builds a fresh `PluginRegistry(scheduler: scheduler)`.

   Test cases (names verbatim):
   - `'rejects bad namespace'` ‚Äî register `_OrderPlugin('Bad-NS')` ‚Üí `throwsArgumentError`.
   - `'rejects duplicate namespace'` ‚Äî register `'a'` twice ‚Üí second `throwsStateError`.
   - `'prefixes tool names and rejects dotted names'` ‚Äî `_OrderPlugin('router', toolNames:['navigate_to'])` ‚Üí `mergedTools().keys` contains `'router.navigate_to'`. Fresh registry with dotted tool name `'a.b'` ‚Üí `throwsArgumentError` from `mergedTools()`.
   - `'rejects intra-plugin tool collision'` ‚Äî `_OrderPlugin('x', toolNames:['t','t'])` ‚Üí `mergedTools()` `throwsStateError`.
   - `'preserves registration order across all dispatches'` ‚Äî register `'a'` then `'b'`; run all dispatches with `const ObservationContext(turn:0, sinceLastAction:Duration.zero)` and `const ExecutedAction(toolName:'core.tap', args:{}, result:ToolResult(ok:true))`; expect `log == ['init:a','init:b','obs:a','obs:b','busy:a','busy:b','act:a','act:b','dispose:a','dispose:b']`.
   - `'observe fragment passes unknown fields through'` ‚Äî `out['a']` equals `{'k':1, 'unknown_future_field':'opaque'}`.
   - `'per-method exception isolation and 3-strikes auto-disable'` ‚Äî register `_ThrowOnObserve('x')` and `_OrderPlugin('y')`; loop `observeAll` 5√ó; each turn `out` lacks `'x'` and contains `'y'`; assert `log.where((s)=>s=='obs:x').length == 3`.
   - `'dispose isolation runs every plugin'` ‚Äî register `_ThrowOnDispose('a')` then `_OrderPlugin('b')`; `disposeAll`; expect `log == ['dispose:a','dispose:b']`.

   Test: `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/registry_test.dart` ‚Üí all PASS.

   Commit: `git commit -am "test(contract): registry validation, ordering, isolation, auto-disable"`

7. PluginContext tests ‚Äî `exploration_flutter/test/contract/plugin_context_test.dart`

   Helper `_HandlerPlugin extends ExplorationPlugin({namespace, handlers})` ‚Äî `initialize` registers each via `c.registerErrorHandler`; remaining methods no-op. Setup: same as step 6.

   Test cases:
   - `'extension method is auto-namespaced'` ‚Äî `expect(PluginContext.buildExtensionMethodName('router','ping'), 'ext.flutter.exploration.router.ping')`.
   - `'error handlers run in order; first true short-circuits'` ‚Äî register three `_HandlerPlugin`s with handlers `[a‚Üífalse, b‚Üítrue, c‚Üítrue]` that append `'a'/'b'/'c'` to `calls`; after `initializeAll`, `r.dispatchError(FlutterErrorDetails(exception: StateError('x')))` returns `true` and `calls == ['a','b']`.
   - `'frame callback forwards to scheduler'` ‚Äî build `PluginContext(namespace:'a', scheduler: SchedulerBinding.instance)`, register `(_) { fired = true; }`; call `SchedulerBinding.instance.scheduleFrame(); await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds:16));` and assert `fired == true`.

   Test: `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/plugin_context_test.dart` ‚Üí all PASS.

   Commit: `git commit -am "test(contract): error chaining, frame forwarding, extension namespacing"`

## Touches

**Files (all created):**
- `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/{types,plugin,plugin_context,registry}.dart`
- `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` (public barrel)
- `exploration_flutter/test/contract/{registry_test,plugin_context_test}.dart`

**Symbols exposed via `package:exploration_flutter/contract.dart`:**
- Interfaces: `ExplorationPlugin`, `ExplorationTool`
- Value types: `JsonSchema`, `ToolResult`, `BusyState` (+ `BusyState.idle`), `ObservationContext`, `ExecutedAction`
- `PluginContext` ‚Äî `{namespace, registerErrorHandler, registerExtension, registerFrameCallback, buildExtensionMethodName}`
- Typedefs: `ErrorHandler`, `ExtensionHandler`
- `PluginRegistry` ‚Äî `register`, `mergedTools`/`finalize`, `initializeAll`, `observeAll`, `busyStateAll`, `onActionExecutedAll`, `disposeAll`, `dispatchError`

## Validation Plan

- [ ] Types + barrel compile ‚Üí `dart analyze exploration_flutter/` ‚Üí `No issues found!`
- [ ] All registry/context tests pass ‚Üí `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/` ‚Üí 11 tests PASS, mapping 1:1 to acceptance criteria as follows:
  - `rejects bad namespace` ‚Üí namespace regex enforcement.
  - `rejects duplicate namespace` ‚Üí uniqueness enforcement.
  - `prefixes tool names and rejects dotted names` ‚Üí mandatory `<ns>.<tool>` prefixing + bare-token rule.
  - `rejects intra-plugin tool collision` ‚Üí tool-name collision rejection at startup.
  - `preserves registration order across all dispatches` ‚Üí registration-order across `mergedTools`/`observe`/`busyState`/`onActionExecuted`/`dispose`.
  - `observe fragment passes unknown fields through` ‚Üí PRD ¬ß7.7 opaque pass-through.
  - `per-method exception isolation and 3-strikes auto-disable` ‚Üí per-method isolation + auto-disable + log line.
  - `dispose isolation runs every plugin` ‚Üí exception isolation in dispose path.
  - `extension method is auto-namespaced` ‚Üí `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<suffix>` rule.
  - `error handlers run in order; first true short-circuits` ‚Üí chained error handler API.
  - `frame callback forwards to scheduler` ‚Üí frame callback hook.
- [ ] `initializeAll` exception isolation ‚Üí verified by code review of `registry.dart` (try/catch around `e.plugin.initialize` sets `initFailed` and continues; subsequent `_guard` calls short-circuit).
- [ ] v2 extensibility documented ‚Üí `grep -q "v2 extension" exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` ‚Üí match.
- [ ] Versioning posture documented ‚Üí `grep -q "Versioning (PRD ¬ß7.7)" exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` ‚Üí match.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Public types compile in `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/`: `ExplorationPlugin` (abstract), `ExplorationTool`, `BusyState`, `ObservationContext`, `ExecutedAction`, `ToolResult`, `JsonSchema`, `PluginContext`, `ErrorHandler`, `ExtensionHandler`
- [ ] Library barrel `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` re-exports the public contract surface; `dart analyze` is clean
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.register()` rejects a namespace that does not match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` with `ArgumentError`
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.register()` rejects a duplicate namespace with `StateError` carrying the offending namespace
- [ ] Tools are auto-prefixed: a plugin whose `namespace = "router"` and tool `name = "navigate_to"` exposes `router.navigate_to` in the merged tool list; `PluginRegistry.mergedTools()` rejects a tool whose name is not a bare token (contains a `.`) with `ArgumentError`
- [ ] Two plugins contributing the same fully-qualified tool name cause `PluginRegistry.finalize()` to throw `StateError` at startup
- [ ] Registration order is preserved across `mergedTools()`, `observeAll()`, `busyStateAll()`, `onActionExecutedAll()`, `disposeAll()` ‚Äî verified by an order-tracking fake plugin test
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerExtension("ping", handler)` registers the VM service extension as `ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.ping`; the underlying `developer.registerExtension` receives the fully-qualified name
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerErrorHandler` stores handlers in registration order; `PluginRegistry.dispatchError(details)` calls them in order and stops at the first handler that returns `true`
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerFrameCallback` forwards to `SchedulerBinding.addPostFrameCallback` (validated via fake binding capturing the call)
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.initializeAll()` calls each plugin's `initialize()` in registration order; an exception in one plugin's `initialize()` is logged and the plugin is marked failed but the session continues
- [ ] Each per-method dispatch (`observe`, `busyState`, `onActionExecuted`) wraps the call in try/catch ‚Äî an exception is logged with the plugin namespace and the call returns the per-method "no contribution" sentinel (null fragment, `BusyState.idle`, no-op respectively) without propagating
- [ ] After 3 consecutive failures of the same method, the plugin is marked auto-disabled for that method for the rest of the session and a single `[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>` log line is emitted; further calls are skipped
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.disposeAll()` is exception-isolated and runs every plugin's `dispose()` even if earlier ones throw
- [ ] Unfamiliar fields in a plugin's observation fragment are passed through unchanged (host does not strip unknown keys) ‚Äî verified by a test asserting structural equality on a fragment containing future keys
- [ ] `ExplorationPlugin` is shaped so adding `List<ExplorationResource> get resources => const []` and `List<ExplorationPrompt> get prompts => const []` would be source-compatible (default-implemented getters); a documentation comment in the source records this constraint

title: ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, and conflict detection
description: Host binding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding. ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...]) called from main(), kDebugMode/profile gating, hard error if another custom binding (IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding etc.) is already installed, registers the 'ext.flutter.exploration.*' core namespace and a handshake/ping extension. Defines the VM service extension naming convention used by core and namespaced for plugins. PRD ¬ß6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding` and installs itself as the singleton `WidgetsBinding.instance` when `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...])` is called from `main()` in debug or profile mode
- [ ] `ensureInitialized` throws `StateError` with a message naming the conflicting binding's runtime type if a non-`ExplorationBinding` `WidgetsBinding` is already installed (e.g. `IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding`)
- [ ] `ensureInitialized` is a no-op (returns without installing the binding, returns `null`, and does NOT register any VM service extensions) outside debug/profile mode (when both `kDebugMode` and `kProfileMode` are false)
- [ ] The reserved namespace `ext.flutter.exploration.*` is documented in the package README with the format `ext.flutter.exploration.<core_or_plugin_namespace>.<suffix>` and the rule that `core` is reserved for host-owned extensions
- [ ] A handshake VM service extension is registered at `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` and returns `{protocolVersion: '1', bindingType: 'ExplorationBinding', flutterMode: 'debug' | 'profile', pluginCount: <int>}`
- [ ] The user-supplied `plugins` list is stored verbatim on the binding (preserves order, no de-duplication) and is exposed to the registry via an internal accessor that cx6.3 will consume
- [ ] Calling `ensureInitialized` twice in the same process returns the same instance and does NOT re-register extensions or throw

title: Repo & multi-package scaffolding
description: Lay down the v1 package layout and CI baseline. Packages: exploration_flutter (host binding), exploration_agent (harness library, web-compatible ‚Äî NO dart:io), exploration_cli (CLI frontend, where dart:io may live), exploration_devtools (DevTools extension, Flutter web app), exploration_router (reference plugin), exploration_riverpod (reference plugin), exploration_dio (reference plugin). Shared lints/analysis_options, baseline CI (analyze + test) across the workspace. Blocks every downstream story.
design:
## Design Summary
Dart 3.6+ workspace with seven packages ‚Äî `exploration_flutter` (Flutter host binding), `exploration_agent` (pure-Dart web-compatible harness), `exploration_cli` (Dart CLI, may use `dart:io`), `exploration_devtools` (Flutter web app on `package:devtools_extensions`), and three reference plugins (`exploration_router`/`_riverpod`/`_dio`). Shared root `analysis_options.yaml` included by every package. Baseline GitHub Actions CI runs `dart analyze`, an `exploration_agent` `dart:io`-free grep gate, and per-package tests on PRs.

Key invariants: SDK floors Dart >= 3.11.0 (all) and Flutter >= 3.41.0 (Flutter packages); `exploration_agent/lib/` MUST be `dart:io`-free per PRD ¬ß6.3 / ¬ß22 (harness runs in DevTools web app); `exploration_devtools` pins `devtools_extensions: ^0.4.0` per ¬ß22 (DevTools version-skew). No code beyond green analyze + one trivial test per package ‚Äî reference plugins are scaffolds only (real implementations land in cx6.25/.26/.27). Single-package layout, pubspec auto-discovery, and Melos are explicitly ruled out (PRD ¬ß6/¬ß18; native Dart workspaces suffice). `dart:io` usage belongs in `exploration_cli` or behind DTD in `exploration_devtools` (cx6.21).

## Implementation Plan

1. Root workspace + lints ‚Äî `pubspec.yaml`, `analysis_options.yaml`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: lenny_workspace
   publish_to: none
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   workspace:
     - packages/exploration_flutter
     - packages/exploration_agent
     - packages/exploration_cli
     - packages/exploration_devtools
     - packages/exploration_router
     - packages/exploration_riverpod
     - packages/exploration_dio
   ```

   `analysis_options.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   include: package:lints/recommended.yaml
   analyzer:
     language: { strict-casts: true, strict-inference: true, strict-raw-types: true }
   linter:
     rules: [prefer_single_quotes, sort_pub_dependencies, unawaited_futures, avoid_print]
   ```

   Test (paired with step 2): `dart pub get` ‚Üí exit 0.

   Commit: `chore: initialize Dart workspace with shared lints`

2. `exploration_agent` (pure Dart, web-compatible) ‚Äî `packages/exploration_agent/`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_agent
   description: Web-compatible harness library.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   dependencies: { meta: ^1.16.0 }
   dev_dependencies: { lints: ^5.1.0, test: ^1.25.0 }
   ```

   `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   `lib/exploration_agent.dart`:
   ```dart
   /// Web-compatible harness library. MUST NOT import dart:io.
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_agent';
   ```

   `test/smoke_test.dart`:
   ```dart
   import 'package:exploration_agent/exploration_agent.dart';
   import 'package:test/test.dart';
   void main() {
     test('package loads', () => expect(packageName, equals('exploration_agent')));
   }
   ```

   Test: `dart pub get && dart analyze packages/exploration_agent && dart test packages/exploration_agent` ‚Üí analyze `No issues found!`, `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): scaffold web-compatible harness package`

3. `exploration_flutter` (Flutter host binding) ‚Äî `packages/exploration_flutter/`

   `flutter create --template=package --no-pub packages/exploration_flutter`. Overwrite `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_flutter
   description: Host WidgetsBinding for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0, flutter: ">=3.41.0" }
   dependencies:
     flutter: { sdk: flutter }
     exploration_agent: any
     meta: ^1.16.0
   dev_dependencies:
     flutter_test: { sdk: flutter }
     lints: ^5.1.0
   ```

   Replace `lib/exploration_flutter.dart`:
   ```dart
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_flutter';
   ```

   Replace `test/exploration_flutter_test.dart` with a `flutter_test` test asserting `packageName == 'exploration_flutter'` (`import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';`). Add `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && flutter test packages/exploration_flutter` ‚Üí `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_flutter): scaffold host binding package`

4. `exploration_cli` (Dart CLI) ‚Äî `packages/exploration_cli/`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_cli
   description: CLI frontend for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   dependencies: { exploration_agent: any, args: ^2.6.0 }
   dev_dependencies: { lints: ^5.1.0, test: ^1.25.0 }
   ```

   `bin/exploration_cli.dart`:
   ```dart
   import 'dart:io';
   void main(List<String> arguments) {
     stdout.writeln('exploration_cli v0 ‚Äî scaffold');
   }
   ```

   `lib/exploration_cli.dart`:
   ```dart
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_cli';
   ```

   `test/smoke_test.dart`: same shape as step 2 asserting `packageName == 'exploration_cli'`. `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && dart analyze packages/exploration_cli && dart test packages/exploration_cli` ‚Üí analyze clean, `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_cli): scaffold CLI frontend package`

5. `exploration_devtools` (Flutter web, DevTools extension) ‚Äî `packages/exploration_devtools/`

   `flutter create --template=app --platforms=web --no-pub packages/exploration_devtools`. Overwrite `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_devtools
   description: DevTools extension for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace

   dependencies:
   ```
